raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/year>, I rented a XXXX XXXX at XXXX XXXX XXXX through XXXX and returned it on time before my flight. Weeks later, I was shocked to find a charge of {$1300.00} for a completely different rental ( a XXXX in XXXX  ) tied to Rental Agreement # XXXX a rental that was not mine. 

I immediately contacted XXXX XXXX who admitted multiple times that the charge was a mistake. Despite this, I was forced into a cycle of endless calls, broken promises, and contradictory information. Over nearly XXXX months, I received only partial credits ( {$210.00} and {$480.00} ), leaving {$690.00} still unpaid. Each time I was told the refund was coming, another error occurred. 

As a responsible cardholder, I disputed the charge with American Express, but they denied my claim even though the companys own records confirm I was wrongly billed. I feel abandoned caught between a rental car company that admits the error but refuses to make me whole, and a credit card issuer that failed to protect me. 

No consumer should have to spend months fighting to get money back for services never received. I am retired and living with health challenges, and this process has been exhausting and deeply unfair. If XXXX can bill someone for a car they never rented and Amex wont protect its customers, what protections do consumers really have? 

I am asking the CFPB to hold these companies accountable and to ensure I am refunded the remaining {$690.00}.
